---
title: "About Foundation Stack"
---

## What this site is

**Foundation Stack** is a **continuously updated** publication on **aerobic exercise prescription for adolescents with persistent post-concussive symptoms (PPCS)**. Each installment follows the same structure: problem → comparison table → disagreements → methods → clinical SOP → limitations.

It is a **dedicated public series** at [fs.guanglab.org](https://fs.guanglab.org/) — separate from personal-site tools on [guanglab.org](https://guanglab.org/) and from any private research operations.

## Who it is for

- Athletic trainers and physical therapists (primary)  
- Sport medicine and rehabilitation researchers (primary)  
- Parents and athletes navigating prolonged recovery (start from each installment’s **SOP** and plain-language notes)  

## Four pillars

| Code | Prefix | Focus |
|------|--------|--------|
| `reproduce` | `pillar1-` | Landmark trial dose–outcome logic |
| `methods` | `pillar2-` | Protocol and measurement comparison |
| `synth` | `pillar3-` | Priors, dashboards, thought experiments |
| `sop` | `pillar4-` | Pitfalls and Monday-morning checklists |

Installment folders: `posts/pillar{N}-{seq}-{slug}/`.

## Author

**Guang Li (Jack)** — graduate researcher in athletic training, Idaho State University. Personal site: [guanglab.org](https://guanglab.org/).

## Privacy note

This site is **public**. Internal dissertation workflows, advisor materials, private research operations, and restricted project hubs are **not** linked here.

## Subscribe

Use the **RSS feed** linked from the [series hub](/index.html) listing page.
